title: SCBE coding agent Qwen Stage 6 repair v12 (asymmetric forced-prefix)
description: v12 retry after v11 gate failed 0/5 (regression vs v10's 2/5). v11 root cause: forced-prefix shards covered all 5 kinds and disrupted the two prompts (hex_trace, cost_propagation) that v10 was already passing. v12 hypothesis: ASYMMETRIC reinforcement. Keep v10's exact base corpus (594 rows: stage6 + stage6_repair + must_pass_boost). Add forced-prefix only for the 3 prompts that v10 failed (resource_jump_cancel, lane_separation, training_boundary), with 3x duplication = 216 rows. Total ~810 rows; forced-prefix concentration 26.7%. Skip forced-prefix for hex_trace and cost_propagation entirely so v10's working signal on those stays untouched. v11 jump_cancel went 4/7 missing -> 1/7 missing under forced-prefix, showing the shape works on prompts that need it. v12 keeps lora_dropout 0.05 / max_steps 150 / seed 62 (fresh seed). Inline gate against stage6_atomic_workflow_eval_contract.json blocks any HF push if score < 0.8 or any must_pass prompt fails. FINAL SFT attempt — if v12 fails, pivot to constrained decoding or DPO, do not run v13.
system_prompt: You are an SCBE-AETHERMOORE GeoSeal command-line coding agent. Preserve token-to-binary/hex flow across code, semantic overlay, structural chemistry frame, and resource-aware workflow composition. Keep material chemistry separate from structural chemistry templates, predict resource overruns before commit, and use steady-state fallback plus re-advance when a launch would exceed budget.

evaluation.notes: Final SFT iteration. Tests asymmetric reinforcement: forced-prefix only on the 3 failing kinds, hex_trace + cost_propagation left under v10's working signal. If gate still fails, next pivot is constrained decoding (token-level prefix injection at inference) or DPO with v10/v11 outputs as REJECTED — NOT v13.
